scenario: A local macOS app shell lets a user search Codex/OpenClaw/future Claude Code sessions through the same public-safe recall surfaces, inspect safe summaries and source refs, filter by project/status/priority, and hand high-signal refs to an agent without raw transcript rendering.
expected_public_safe_evidence: query string with no private transcript text
project, status, and priority filter state
blocker codes for unavailable local DB or plugin tools
pass_criteria: User can search indexed sessions and see safe summaries with source refs.
User can filter results by project, status, priority, and blocker state.
User can copy source refs and selected public-safe summary text for an OpenClaw agent.
User can request bounded expansion through existing loo_* profiles before more detail is shown.
The shell must fail closed when local DB access, required plugin tools, or OpenClaw plugin loading is unavailable.
CUA and Peekaboo appear as readiness/status surfaces only unless a future backend-specific approval gate is proven.
fail_criteria: UI renders raw Codex transcripts or raw prompts.
UI captures screenshots or videos as public evidence.
UI performs live GUI mutation or live Codex control without explicit approval.
UI claims one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, unattended takeover, or release-ready macOS app status.
UI falls back to raw file reads when plugin tools or the local DB are unavailable.
known_gaps: No local macOS app shell has been built in this issue.
No signed or notarized app artifact has been produced.
No live OpenClaw gateway UI smoke has been run.
No CUA no-focus proof or Peekaboo snapshot safety proof has been produced.
Claude Code remains a future adapter boundary, not a proved equivalent adapter.
next_action: Build a thin local shell only after CLI, MCP, and OpenClaw gateway paths prove the underlying recall loop and this scorecard has a public-safe run packet.
proof_boundary: This advisory local public-safe beta scorecard does not prove a signed or release-ready macOS app, one-click install, Claude Code adapter parity, live Codex control, GUI mutation, or safe desktop automation.
